summary: postinstall exfiltrates AWS EC2 IMDSv2 credentials to an ngrok tunnel
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall script queries the EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 using IMDSv2, retrieves the IAM role name and its temporary security credentials from /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, and POSTs the credentials to a hardcoded ngrok tunnel host (crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev) with a '?real_aws_keys=1' query flag. The package has no declared purpose, no description, and the destination is unrelated to any legitimate functionality. Any installer running on an EC2 instance with an attached IAM role will have short-lived AWS credentials stolen at install time, granting the attacker access to the installer's AWS account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
